title: ConfigurationJointSnaps Object
description: Collection object that provides access to all the joint snaps that have been defined for a ConfigurationJointSnapColumn. You can also use this collection to define new joint snaps that will then be available when specifying which snap to use in a cell.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds a new snap to the column. The snaps associated with the column can be used in the cells in the column.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: A method that returns the specified snap using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: A method that returns the snap with the specified name.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of snaps for the column.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: ConfigurationJointSnaps.add Method
add.description: Adds a new snap to the column. The snaps associated with the column can be used in the cells in the column.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the new snap. The name must be unique with respect to the other snaps defined for this column. An empty string can be provided, which will cause Fusion to use a default naming scheme to create a name.

parameters.name: jointGeometry
parameters.description: A JointGeometry object that defines how the snap is defined. When creating the JointGeometry object, it must be limited to geometry in the occurrence associated with the column.

classType.title: ConfigurationJointSnaps.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: ConfigurationJointSnaps.item Method
item.description: A method that returns the specified snap using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified snap or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the snap to return, where the first row is index 0.

itemByName.title: ConfigurationJointSnaps.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: A method that returns the snap with the specified name.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified snap or null if a snap with the specified name does not exist.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the snap to return.

count.title: ConfigurationJointSnaps.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of snaps for the column.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationJointSnaps_var.count

isValid.title: ConfigurationJointSnaps.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationJointSnaps_var.isValid

objectType.title: ConfigurationJointSnaps.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationJointSnaps_var.objectType
